description:
You are the on-call database-reliability engineer.  
    A week’s worth of daily backup status logs already lives in  

        /home/user/backups/logs  

    Each file is named  

        backup_status_YYYY-MM-DD.log  

    and every line inside a file has the exact structure  

        host HOSTNAME: RESULT (additional-info)  

    • HOSTNAME is the lowercase database host (e.g. db1).  
    • RESULT is either the uppercase word SUCCESS or FAILED.  
    • "(additional-info)" can be anything and may contain spaces.

    Your job is to isolate every failure that happened in the last seven
    log files (they are the only files in that directory) and prepare two
    artefacts in a *new* directory:

        /home/user/backups/reports

    1. failure_summary.csv  
       • This **MUST** be comma-separated with **no extra whitespace**.  
       • First line is a header exactly:  
             Host,Failures  
       • One line per host, sorted alphabetically by host name.  
       • After the per-host lines, add a final summary line that begins
         with the word “Total” and gives the grand total of failures.  
       • Example of the required overall layout (values will be checked
         verbatim by the grader):

             Host,Failures
             db1,3
             db2,3
             db3,2
             Total,8

       • The file must end with a single trailing newline (Unix LF).

    2. failure_events.log  
       • One line for *each* failed backup.  
       • Lines have to be in chronological order (oldest first).  
       • The format of every line must be:

             YYYY-MM-DD <single-space> <original-log-line>

         For example (again, the grader will check the full contents):

             2023-08-01 host db2: FAILED (error: timeout)

       • Finish the file with a single trailing newline.

    Additional constraints
    ----------------------
    • Create /home/user/backups/reports if it doesn’t exist.  
    • Work only with standard GNU/Linux command-line tools available in
      a minimal distribution (grep, awk, sort, cut, etc.).  
    • Do *not* alter, remove or rename the existing log files.  
    • Do *not* hard-code the number of failures; derive everything from
      the log contents.  
    • When you’re done, you should be able to run

          cat /home/user/backups/reports/failure_summary.csv
          cat /home/user/backups/reports/failure_events.log

      and see exactly the data shown above.

    A quick self-check you can do before finishing:
      ‑ There are eight total failure lines across the seven files.
      ‑ db1 and db2 each failed three times; db3 failed twice.
      ‑ The first failure chronologically is on 2023-08-01, the last on
        2023-08-07.

    Submit after both required files exist with the precise contents
    and formatting described.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data for automated verification.
        These seven files already exist before the task starts.
    -->
    <existingLogs base="/home/user/backups/logs">
        <file name="backup_status_2023-08-01.log"><![CDATA[
host db1: SUCCESS (duration: 122s)
host db2: FAILED (error: timeout)
host db3: SUCCESS (duration: 130s)
]]></file>
        <file name="backup_status_2023-08-02.log"><![CDATA[
host db1: FAILED (error: disk full)
host db2: FAILED (error: timeout)
host db3: SUCCESS (duration: 128s)
]]></file>
        <file name="backup_status_2023-08-03.log"><![CDATA[
host db1: SUCCESS (duration: 120s)
host db2: SUCCESS (duration: 121s)
host db3: SUCCESS (duration: 125s)
]]></file>
        <file name="backup_status_2023-08-04.log"><![CDATA[
host db1: FAILED (error: network)
host db2: SUCCESS (duration: 118s)
host db3: FAILED (error: disk full)
]]></file>
        <file name="backup_status_2023-08-05.log"><![CDATA[
host db1: SUCCESS (duration: 119s)
host db2: SUCCESS (duration: 117s)
host db3: SUCCESS (duration: 124s)
]]></file>
        <file name="backup_status_2023-08-06.log"><![CDATA[
host db1: FAILED (error: timeout)
host db2: FAILED (error: disk full)
host db3: SUCCESS (duration: 123s)
]]></file>
        <file name="backup_status_2023-08-07.log"><![CDATA[
host db1: SUCCESS (duration: 118s)
host db2: SUCCESS (duration: 116s)
host db3: FAILED (error: network)
]]></file>
    </existingLogs>

    <!--
        The grader will look for these two files and verify the
        *exact* byte-for-byte contents.
    -->
    <expectedReports base="/home/user/backups/reports">
        <file name="failure_summary.csv"><![CDATA[
Host,Failures
db1,3
db2,3
db3,2
Total,8
]]></file>

        <file name="failure_events.log"><![CDATA[
2023-08-01 host db2: FAILED (error: timeout)
2023-08-02 host db1: FAILED (error: disk full)
2023-08-02 host db2: FAILED (error: timeout)
2023-08-04 host db1: FAILED (error: network)
2023-08-04 host db3: FAILED (error: disk full)
2023-08-06 host db1: FAILED (error: timeout)
2023-08-06 host db2: FAILED (error: disk full)
2023-08-07 host db3: FAILED (error: network)
]]></file>
    </expectedReports>
